facts.text: Old Bethpage’s name comes from Quaker Thomas Powell, who named the area after the Biblical town Bethphage between Jericho and Jerusalem.
The Long Island Rail Road first stopped at a station called Jerusalem Station in 1851, later renamed Bethpage station, and still serves the town today.
The Old Bethpage Village Restoration opened in 1970, preserving 18th‑ and 19th‑century buildings and offering interactive exhibits that bring Long Island’s rural past to life.
From 1936 to 1994, Grumman Aircraft Engineering Corporation operated in Bethpage, building the F‑14 Tomcat, the Apollo Lunar Excursion Module, and more.
Old Bethpage’s historic village hosts community events like the Long Island Fair, strengthening its role as a center for public history and cultural engagement.
